title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Docker Build Room
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working container release test and fault matrix for dockerfile and compose validation, image tests, fault injection, and quality gates, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of container release test and fault matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Build Checks Lint
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Build Checks Lint.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-1c6t1h". Tailor its container-test-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Clean Host Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer, keep Docker Clean Host Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-1c6t1h
  - competency: docker-clean-host-transfer
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: clean host transfer
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: shutdown duration exit state and durable-work reconciliation
    case-key: 1c6t1h
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Clean Host Transfer structural check is only one gate. container release test and fault matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Clean Host Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Clean Host Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: A local cached success proves another host can pull and reproduce the release.
